lede: A look at June 10 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Alexandra Stan and Susanne Albers, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Kidnapping of Jaycee Dugard
events.desc: Eleven-year-old Jaycee Lee Dugard is kidnapped in South Lake Tahoe, California; she would remain a captive until 2009.

events.title: June Democratic Struggle
events.desc: June Democratic Struggle: The June Democratic Struggle starts in South Korea, and people protest against the government.

events.title: John Diefenbaker
events.desc: John Diefenbaker leads the Progressive Conservative Party of Canada to a stunning upset in the 1957 Canadian federal election, ending 22 years of Liberal Party government.

events.title: Bob Smith (doctor)
events.desc: Dr. Robert Smith takes his last drink, and Alcoholics Anonymous is founded in Akron, Ohio, United States, by him and Bill Wilson.

events.title: Mount Tarawera
events.desc: Mount Tarawera in New Zealand erupts, killing 153 people and burying the famous Pink and White Terraces. Eruptions continue for three months creating a large, 17 km (11 mi) long fissure across the mountain peak.

events.title: League of Prizren
events.desc: League of Prizren is established, to oppose the decisions of the Congress of Berlin and the Treaty of San Stefano, as a consequence of which the Albanian lands in the Balkans were being partitioned and given to the neighbor states of Serbia, Montenegro, Bulgaria, and Greece.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Big Bethel: Confederate troops under John B. Magruder defeat a much larger Union force led by General Ebenezer W. Pierce in Virginia.

events.title: Rama I
events.desc: King Buddha Yodfa Chulaloke (Rama I) of Siam (modern day Thailand) is crowned.

events.title: Thirty Years' War
events.desc: Thirty Years' War: Battle of Záblatí, a turning point in the Bohemian Revolt.

events.title: Third Crusade
events.desc: Third Crusade: Frederick I Barbarossa drowns in the river Saleph while leading an army to Jerusalem.

births.name: Eliot Spitzer
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 54th Governor of New York

deaths.text: Antoni Gaudí, Spanish architect, designed the Park Güell
Bridget Bishop, Colonial Massachusetts woman hanged as a witch during the Salem witch trials
Emperor Daizong of Tang, Emperor of China from 762 to 779

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Diana degli Andalò<ref name="Martyrologium Romanum">
Christian feast day: Blessed Edward Poppe<ref name="Martyrologium Romanum">
Christian feast day: Getulius, Amancius and Cerealus
Christian feast day: Guardian Angel of Portugal
Christian feast day: John of Tobolsk (Russian Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Landry of Paris<ref name="Martyrologium Romanum">
